arts.ttl: Roma and Parma hoping to get back on their feet
arts.sum: Roma have won 61% of their Serie A matches against Parma (W33 D10 L11) and among the teams they have faced at least 40 times in the competition the Giallorossi only have a better win rate against Bari (65%). Roma have lost eight league games so far this season and in the first half of a Serie A campaign, only in 1950-51 did the Giallorossi suffer more defeats (11) – that is also the only season in which the Romans lost more than eight of the first 17 matches (9).

arts.ttl: Milan’s Bennacer returns to group training, eyes Roma clash
arts.sum: AC Milan received a boost at their Milanello training ground as Ismael Bennacer returned to group training, signaling his imminent comeback after a lengthy injury layoff. The Algerian midfielder has been sidelined for over three months due to a grade three calf injury sustained while on international duty. The severity of the issue led to surgery in Finland on September 16, performed by Dr. Lasse Lempainen. Now, with the worst behind him, Bennacer is close to being fully available for manager Paulo Fonseca. arts.ttl: Milan’s Leao suffers thigh flexor strain: Out for Roma clash, Supercoppa in doubt
arts.sum: AC Milan's star forward Rafael Leao faces a spell on the sidelines after sustaining a thigh injury during the recent match against Verona. The Portuguese international's game at the Bentegodi stadium lasted only 32 minutes before he was replaced by Theo Hernandez due to a muscular issue. arts.sum: Roma manager Claudio Ranieri has admitted he will be looking at Everton in a different light ahead of January, now that the Friedkin family have officially taken charge at Goodison Park. arts.ttl: Friedkins arrive at Everton while facing flak at Roma after bright start
arts.sum: American father and son began positively at the Stadio Olimpico but botched managerial decisions soured mood A banner raised by Roma supporters before their Coppa Italia game at home to Sampdoria on Wednesday spoke to the prevailing mood. “Club and players: all a bunch of frauds,” it read. By recent standards, that was rather tame. The Friedkin Group, which completed its takeover of Everton on Thursday, has owned Roma since 2020 and enjoyed notable successes: hiring José Mourinho as manager and delivering him the squad he needed to win the first Europa Conference League, as well as finishing as Europa League runners-up a year later. However, the decision to fire the Portuguese in January divided opinion among the fanbase, and the sacking of his successor, Daniele De Rossi, in September sparked open revolt. Continue reading...
